pattern: Any palindrome built this way peels apart into an outer shell of mirrored pairs straddling the join between the two pieces, plus an inner core that must be a palindrome living entirely inside one piece, so trying every possible shell depth and combining it with the best inner core from either side finds the optimum.
hints: Picture the final palindrome split at the join between the s-piece and the t-piece. The characters right at that join must mirror each other outward: the last character of the s-piece must match the first character of the t-piece, the second-to-last must match the second, and so on.

For every possible starting point in s and ending point in t, precompute how many such mirrored pairs you can chain before a mismatch or running out of characters -- this gives, for every pair of anchor points, a maximum 'shell' depth.

Once the shell of depth k is fixed, whatever is left over must itself be a palindrome living entirely inside s (starting right after the shell) or entirely inside t (ending right before the shell) -- precompute, for every position, the longest palindrome starting there in s and the longest palindrome ending there in t, then take the best combination over every shell depth.

edge_inputs.description: statement example, a matching single character from each side
statement example, no shared characters at all
statement example, the whole answer lives entirely inside t
statement example, a shell plus an inner core from s
two different single characters, only a length-1 palindrome is possible
the best answer is a palindrome entirely inside s, with t contributing nothing useful
the best answer is a palindrome entirely inside t, with s contributing nothing useful
both strings share structure, favoring a deep mirrored shell across the join

stress.description: random lowercase strings near the maximum length on both sides
stress.generator_python:
def gen(rng, size):
    letters = 'abcdefghijklmnopqrstuvwxyz'
    n = max(1, min(30, size))
    m = max(1, min(30, size))
    s = ''.join(rng.choice(letters) for _ in range(n))
    t = ''.join(rng.choice(letters) for _ in range(m))
    return [s, t]
